name: Cajun Tofu
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Firm tofu, drained and cut into 3/4-inch cubes
1 medium Red bell pepper, finely chopped (about 1/2 cup)
1 medium Green bell pepper, finely chopped (about 1/2 cup)
1 large White onion, finely chopped (1 cup)
1 gallon water (3/4 gallon for thicker soup)
1/2 cup Finely sliced green onion tops
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry tofu in very hot olive or peanut oil until sealed.
2. Pour the oil into a stock pot, adding enough to cover the bottom if necessary.
3. Make a roux by adding an equal amount of flour and cooking until thickened.
4. Add chopped peppers, onion, celery, tomatoes, tomato sauce, water, Worcestershire sauce, salt, red pepper flakes, black pepper, garlic salt, and paprika.
5. Simmer for 25 minutes.
6. Add the drained tofu, place the lid on the pot, and simmer for another 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Add 3 or 4 fresh bay leaves and let cool for 15 minutes.
8. Reheat if necessary and serve over steamed rice. Top with fresh parsley.
9. If a less spicy mix is desired, reduce the amount of red pepper flakes to taste.
